title: Bench top CUT&RUN with antibodies-online™ CUT&RUN Sets
author: Antibodies Online Gmbh

steps.title: » Wash buffer (165 mL) A...
steps.instruction: » Wash buffer (165 mL) A B C 1 Component Volume Final concentration 2 ddH2O 156.7 mL - 3 1 M HEPES pH 7.5 3.3 mL 20 mM 4 5 M NaCl 4.95 mL 150 mM 5 2 M Spermidine 41.25 µL 0.5 mM • Store Wash Buffer without protease inhibitors for up to one week at 4 °C. • Add protease inhibitors fresh before use, e.g.: 1.65 mL Protease Inhibitor (EDTA-free) » Binding Buffer (45 mL) A B C 1 Component Volume Final concentration 2 ddH2O 43.6 mL - 3 1 M HEPES pH 7.5 900 µL 20 mM 4 1 M KCl 450 µL 10 mM 5 1 M CaCl2 45 µL 1 mM 6 2.5 M MnCl2 16 µL 1 mM • Store Binding Buffer for up to six months at 4 °C. » Digitonin Wash Buffer (82.5 mL) A B C 1 Component Volume Final concentration 2 1.2 % Digitonin 3354 µL 0.05 % 3 Wash Buffer 79 mL - • Store Digitonin Wash Buffer for up to one day at 4 °C. • Recommended Digitonin concentration ranges from 0.025% to 0.1%. • The effectiveness of Digitonin varies between batches. Test cell permeability using Trypan Blue to determine the optimal concentration to use. » Antibody Buffer (1.5 mL) A B C 1 Component Volume Final concentration 2 0.5 M EDTA 6 µL 2 mM 3 10 % BSA 15 µL 0.1 % 4 Digitonin Wash Buffer 1.5 mL - • Store Antibody Buffer for up to one day at 4 °C until use. » Low Salt Rinse Buffer (27 mL) A B C 1 Component Volume Final concentration 2 ddH2O 25.3 mL - 3 1 M HEPES pH 7.5 540 µL 20 mM 4 2 M Spermidine 6.75 µL 0.5 mM 5 1.2% Digitonin 1125 µL 0.05 % • Store Low Salt Rinse Buffer for up to one week at 4 °C until use. » Low Salt Incubation Buffer (3 mL) A B C 1 Component Volume Final concentration 2 ddH2O 2.8 mL - 3 1 M HEPES pH 7.5 10.5 µL 3.5 mM 4 1 M CaCl2 30 µL 10 mM 5 1.2% Digitonin 125 µL 0.05% • Store Low Salt Incubation Buffer for up to one week at 4 °C until use. » Low Salt Stop Buffer (3 mL) A B C 1 Component Volume Final concentration 2 ddH2O 2.8 mL - 3 5 M NaCl 102 µL 170 mM 4 0.2 M EGTA 300 µL 20 mM 5 Store Low Salt Stop Buffer at 4 °C until use. 6 Add fresh before use: 7 1.2 % Digitonin 125 µL 0.05% 8 RNase A (10 mg/mL) 15 µL 50 µg/mL 9 Glycogen (20 mg/mL) 7.5 µL 25 µg/mL 10 Optional: 11 heterologous spike-in DNA - 100 pg/mL

steps.title: Harvest 10,000 to 500,000 cells for...
steps.instruction: Harvest 10,000 to 500,000 cells for each sample at Room temperature . Keep cells for each sample in separate tubes.

steps.title: Centrifuge cell solution 00:03:00 at 600...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge cell solution 00:03:00 at 600 x g, °C at Room temperature . Remove the liquid carefully.

steps.title: Gently resuspend cells in 1 mL...
steps.instruction: Gently resuspend cells in 1 mL Wash Buffer by pipetting and transfer cell solution to a 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Centrifuge cell solution 00:03:00 at 600...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge cell solution 00:03:00 at 600 x g at Room temperature and discard the supernatant.

steps.title: Repeat steps 4-5 thrice for a total of four washes
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 4-5 thrice for a total of four washes. go to step #4

steps.title: Resuspend cell pellet for each sample...
steps.instruction: Resuspend cell pellet for each sample in 1 mL Wash Buffer by gently pipetting.

steps.title: Prepare one 1
steps.instruction: Prepare one 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube for each sample.

steps.title: Gently resuspend the antibodies-online™ CUT&RUN Concanavalin...
steps.instruction: Gently resuspend the antibodies-online™ CUT&RUN Concanavalin A Beads antibodies-online Catalog #ABIN6923139

steps.title: Pipette 10 µL CUT&RUN Concanavalin A...
steps.instruction: Pipette 10 µL CUT&RUN Concanavalin A Beads slurry for each sample into the 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tubes.

steps.title: Place the tubes on a magnet...
steps.instruction: Place the tubes on a magnet stand until the fluid is clear. Remove the liquid carefully.

steps.title: Remove the microcentrifuge tube from the magnet stand
steps.instruction: Remove the microcentrifuge tube from the magnet stand.

steps.title: Pipette 1 mL Binding Buffer into...
steps.instruction: Pipette 1 mL Binding Buffer into each tube and resuspend CUT&RUN Concanavalin A Beads by gentle pipetting.

steps.title: Spin down the liquid from the...
steps.instruction: Spin down the liquid from the lid with a quick pulse in a table-top centrifuge (max 100 x g ).

steps.title: Place the tubes on a magnet...
steps.instruction: Place the tubes on a magnet stand until the fluid is clear. Remove the liquid carefully.

steps.title: Remove the microcentrifuge tube from the magnet stand
steps.instruction: Remove the microcentrifuge tube from the magnet stand.

steps.title: Repeat steps 13-16 go to step...
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 13-16 go to step #13 twice for a total of three washes.

steps.title: Gently resuspend the CUT&RUN Concanavalin A...
steps.instruction: Gently resuspend the CUT&RUN Concanavalin A Beads in a volume of Binding Buffer corresponding to the original volume of bead slurry, i.e. 10 µL per sample.

steps.title: Carefully vortex the cell suspension from...
steps.instruction: Carefully vortex the cell suspension from step go to step #7 and add 10 µL of the CUT&RUN Concanavalin A Beads in Binding Buffer prepared in section II to each sample.

steps.title: Close tubes tightly and rotate for...
steps.instruction: Close tubes tightly and rotate for 00:05:00 to 00:10:00 at Room temperature .

steps.title: Place the microcentrifuge tubes on a...
steps.instruction: Place the microcentrifuge tubes on a magnet stand until the fluid is clear. Remove the liquid carefully.

steps.title: Remove the microcentrifuge tubes from the magnet stand
steps.instruction: Remove the microcentrifuge tubes from the magnet stand.

steps.title: Place each tube at a low...
steps.instruction: Place each tube at a low angle on the vortex mixer set to a low speed (approximately 1100 rpm ) and add 100 µL Antibody Buffer containing digitonin .

steps.title: Gently vortex the microcentrifuge tubes until the beads are resuspended
steps.instruction: Gently vortex the microcentrifuge tubes until the beads are resuspended.

steps.title: For the remaining samples, add 1...
steps.instruction: For the remaining samples, add 1 µL primary rabbit antibody - against your protein of interest corresponding to a 1:100 dilution (or a volume corresponding to the manufacturer’s recommended dilution for immunofluorescence).

steps.title: Rotate the microcentrifuge tubes for 00:05:00...
steps.instruction: Rotate the microcentrifuge tubes for 00:05:00 to 00:10:00 at Room temperature or 02:00:00 to Overnight at 4 °C .

steps.title: Spin down the liquid and place...
steps.instruction: Spin down the liquid and place the tubes on a magnet stand until the fluid is clear. Remove the liquid carefully.

steps.title: Remove the microcentrifuge tubes from the magnet stand
steps.instruction: Remove the microcentrifuge tubes from the magnet stand.

steps.title: Resuspend with 1 mL Digitonin Wash...
steps.instruction: Resuspend with 1 mL Digitonin Wash Buffer and mix by inversion. If clumping occurs, gently remove the clumps with a 1 mL pipette tip.

steps.title: Repeat steps 30-32 go to step...
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 30-32 go to step #30 once for a total of two washes. If no secondary antibody is used proceed directly to section VI. pA/G-MNase binding (Step 41).

steps.title: The antibodies-online™ CUT&RUN Secondary antibodies-online Catalog...
steps.instruction: The antibodies-online™ CUT&RUN Secondary antibodies-online Catalog #ABIN6923141 is raised in rabbit against mouse IgG (H&L). In case a primary antibody from a different host species or isotype is used a different secondary must be selected accordingly. Place the tubes on a magnet stand until the fluid is clear. Remove the liquid carefully.

steps.title: Remove the microcentrifuge tubes from the magnet stand
steps.instruction: Remove the microcentrifuge tubes from the magnet stand.

steps.title: Vortex the sample at low speed...
steps.instruction: Vortex the sample at low speed (approximately 1100 rpm ) and add 100 µL Digitonin Wash Buffer per sample along the side of the tube.

steps.title: Tap to remove the remaining beads from the tube side
steps.instruction: Tap to remove the remaining beads from the tube side.

steps.title: Add 1 µL antibodies-online™ CUT&RUN Secondary...
steps.instruction: Add 1 µL antibodies-online™ CUT&RUN Secondary antibodies-online Catalog #ABIN6923141 corresponding to a 1:100 dilution (or a volume corresponding to the manufacturer’s recommended dilution for immunofluorescence).

steps.title: Rotate the microcentrifuge tubes for 01:00:00 at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Rotate the microcentrifuge tubes for 01:00:00 at 4 °C .

steps.title: Spin down the liquid and place...
steps.instruction: Spin down the liquid and place the tubes on a magnet stand until the fluid is clear. Remove the liquid carefully.

steps.title: Remove the microcentrifuge tubes from the magnet stand
steps.instruction: Remove the microcentrifuge tubes from the magnet stand.

steps.title: Resuspend with 1 mL Digitonin Wash...
steps.instruction: Resuspend with 1 mL Digitonin Wash Buffe r and mix by inversion. If clumping occurs, gently remove the clumps with a 1 mL pipette tip.

steps.title: Repeat steps40-42 go to step #36...
steps.instruction: Repeat steps40-42 go to step #36 once for a total of two washes.

steps.title: Place the tubes on a magnet...
steps.instruction: Place the tubes on a magnet stand until the fluid is clear. Remove the liquid carefully.

steps.title: Remove the microcentrifuge tubes from the magnetic stand
steps.instruction: Remove the microcentrifuge tubes from the magnetic stand.

steps.title: Vortex the sample at low speed...
steps.instruction: Vortex the sample at low speed (approximately 1100 rpm, °C ) and add 50 µL Digitonin Wash Buffer per sample along the side of the tube. Add 2.5 µL CUTANA™ pAG-MNase for ChIC/CUT&RUN Assays antibodies-online Catalog #ABIN6950951 Alternatively: Vortex the sample at low speed (approximately 1100 rpm, °C ) and add 150 µL Digitonin Wash Buffer containing 700 ng/mL of your own pA/G-MNase preparation per sample along the side of the tube.

steps.title: Rotate the microcentrifuge tubes for 01:00:00 at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Rotate the microcentrifuge tubes for 01:00:00 at 4 °C .

steps.title: Spin down the liquid and place...
steps.instruction: Spin down the liquid and place the tubes on a magnet stand until the fluid is clear. Remove the liquid carefully.

steps.title: Remove the microcentrifuge tubes from the magnetic stand
steps.instruction: Remove the microcentrifuge tubes from the magnetic stand.

steps.title: Resuspend with 1 mL Digitonin Wash...
steps.instruction: Resuspend with 1 mL Digitonin Wash Buffer and mix by inversion. If clumping occurs, gently remove the clumps with a 1 ml pipette tip.

steps.title: Repeat steps 48-50 go to step...
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 48-50 go to step #48 once for a total of two washes.

steps.title: Spin down the liquid from the...
steps.instruction: Spin down the liquid from the lid with a quick pulse in a table-top centrifuge (max 100 x g, °C ).

steps.title: Place the tubes on a magnet...
steps.instruction: Place the tubes on a magnet stand until the fluid is clear. Remove the liquid carefully.

steps.title: Resuspend with 1 mL Low Salt...
steps.instruction: Resuspend with 1 mL Low Salt Rinse Buffer and mix by inversion. If clumping occurs, gently remove the clumps with a 1 mL pipette tip.

steps.title: Spin down the liquid from the...
steps.instruction: Spin down the liquid from the lid with a quick pulse in a table-top centrifuge (max 100 x g, °C ).

steps.title: Place the tubes on a magnet...
steps.instruction: Place the tubes on a magnet stand until the fluid is clear. Remove the liquid carefully.

steps.title: Repeat steps 54-56 go to step...
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 54-56 go to step #54 once for a total of two washes.

steps.title: Place each tube at a low...
steps.instruction: Place each tube at a low angle on the vortex mixer set to a low speed (approx. 1100 rpm ) and add 200 µL ice cold Low Salt Incubation Buffer per sample along the side of the tube.

steps.title: Incubate tubes at 0 °C for...
steps.instruction: Incubate tubes at 0 °C for the desired time (default is 00:30:00 ).

steps.title: Place the tubes on a cold...
steps.instruction: Place the tubes on a cold magnet stand until the fluid is clear. Remove the liquid carefully.

steps.title: Remove the microcentrifuge tubes from the magnet stand
steps.instruction: Remove the microcentrifuge tubes from the magnet stand.

steps.title: Resuspend with 200 µL Low Salt...
steps.instruction: Resuspend with 200 µL Low Salt Stop Solution and mix by gentle vortexing.

steps.title: Incubate tubes at 37 °C for 00:30:00
steps.instruction: Incubate tubes at 37 °C for 00:30:00 .

steps.title: Place the tubes on a magnet...
steps.instruction: Place the tubes on a magnet stand until the fluid is clear.

steps.title: Transfer the supernatant containing the pA/G-MNase-bound...
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatant containing the pA/G-MNase-bound digested chromatin fragments to fresh 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tubes.

steps.title: Add 2 µL 10% SDS to...
steps.instruction: Add 2 µL 10% SDS to a final concentration of 0.1% and 5 µL Proteinase K ( 10 mg/mL ) to a final concentration of 2.5 mg/mL to each supernatant from step 62.

steps.title: Gently vortex tubes at a low...
steps.instruction: Gently vortex tubes at a low speed of approximately 1100 rpm .

steps.title: Incubate tubes at 50 °C for...
steps.instruction: Incubate tubes at 50 °C for 01:00:00 or at 37 °C Overnight

steps.title: Add 200 µL PCI to tube
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL PCI to tube.

steps.title: Vortex tubes thoroughly at high speed...
steps.instruction: Vortex tubes thoroughly at high speed until the liquid appears milky.

steps.title: Optional
steps.instruction: Optional: Transfer liquid to a phase-lock tube.

steps.title: Centrifuge tubes in a table-top centrifuge...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge tubes in a table-top centrifuge at 16000 x g at 4 °C for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Carefully transfer the upper aqueous phase to a fresh 1
steps.instruction: Carefully transfer the upper aqueous phase to a fresh 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube containing 200 µL Chloroform:Isoamyl Alcohol 24:1 .

steps.title: Vortex tubes thoroughly at high speed...
steps.instruction: Vortex tubes thoroughly at high speed until the liquid appears milky.

steps.title: Centrifuge tubes in a table-top centrifuge...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge tubes in a table-top centrifuge at 16000 x g, °C at 4 °C for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Carefully transfer the upper aqueous phase to a fresh 1
steps.instruction: Carefully transfer the upper aqueous phase to a fresh 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube containing 2 µL glycogen (diluted 1:10 to 2 mg/mL from the 20 mg/mL stock solution).

steps.title: Add 20 µL 3 Molarity (M)...
steps.instruction: Add 20 µL 3 Molarity (M) NaOAc or 100 µL 5 Molarity (M) NH 4 OAc

steps.title: Add 500 µL 100% ethanol
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL 100% ethanol .

steps.title: Place tubes for 00:10:00 in a...
steps.instruction: Place tubes for 00:10:00 in a dry ice/Ethanol mix or Overnight at -20 °C .

steps.title: Centrifuge tubes in a table-top centrifuge...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge tubes in a table-top centrifuge at 16000 x g at 4 °C for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Remove the liquid carefully with a pipette
steps.instruction: Remove the liquid carefully with a pipette.

steps.title: Add 1 mL 70% ethanol
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL 70% ethanol .

steps.title: Centrifuge tubes in a table-top centrifuge...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge tubes in a table-top centrifuge at 16000 x g at 4 °C for 00:01:00 .

steps.title: Remove the liquid carefully with a pipette
steps.instruction: Remove the liquid carefully with a pipette.

steps.title: Air-dry the pellet or dry the pellet in a SpeedVac
steps.instruction: Air-dry the pellet or dry the pellet in a SpeedVac.

steps.title: Dissolve the pellet in 30 µL...
steps.instruction: Dissolve the pellet in 30 µL 1 millimolar (mM) Tris-HCl , 0.1 millimolar (mM) EDTA .

steps.title: Size distribution and concentration of the...
steps.instruction: Size distribution and concentration of the CUT&RUN products can be assessed at this point, e.g. using a Qubit (Flurometer, Invitrogen, Q33228, https://www.thermofisher.com/order/catalog/product/Q33228 , https://s3.amazonaws.com/pr-journal/9de3sqe.jpeg ) or NanoDrop™ 3300 Fluorospectrometer (Fluorospectrometer, NanoDrop™ 3300, nd-3300, https://www.thermofisher.com/order/catalog/product/ND-3300#/ND-3300 , https://s3.amazonaws.com/protocols-files/public/0a4fe9b7a11f73c8ba5166c11a7060d8420e56909f0bef279aef656fabd5f2ab/btsr9xr6.jpg ) or a Bioanalyzer or Tapestation. It is possible that the concentration of the recovered DNA is below the instrument’s detection limit. It is also to be expected that the extracted DNA includes some large DNA fragments that will mask the signal of the CUT&RUN products. In this case it may be useful to PCR-amplify the DNA and check the library on a Bioanalyzer or Tapestation.

steps.title: Prepare the CUT&RUN products sequencing libraries...
steps.instruction: Prepare the CUT&RUN products sequencing libraries according to your established work- flow. Because of the very low background with CUT&RUN, typically 5 million paired-end reads suffice for epitopes with a multitude of genomic binding sites, e.g. transcription factors or nucleosome modifications.

steps.title: The sparse background signal in CUT&RUN...
steps.instruction: The sparse background signal in CUT&RUN samples compared to ChIP-seq samples represents challenge for peak callers that employ statistical models relying on a high sequencing depth and high recall to identify true positives and avoid false positives. In contrast, peak calling for CUT&RUN data sets requires high specificity for true signal peaks. To this end, the Henikoff lab developed the Sparse Enrichments analysis for CUT&RUN (SEACR) peak caller that can be easily accessed using their web server at https://seacr.fredhutch.org/ . Alternatively, the Orkin and Yuan labs have streamlined processing of CUT&RUN data using their CUT&RUNTools pipeline https://bitbucket.org/qzhudfci/cutruntools/

steps.title: For the positive control, add 5...
steps.instruction: For the positive control, add 5 µL CUT&Tag rabbit anti-H3K4me3 IgG Positive Control corresponding to a 1:20 dilution to the corresponding tube. antibodies-online™ CUT&RUN Positive Control antibodies-online Catalog #ABIN6923144

steps.title: For the negative control, add 5...
steps.instruction: For the negative control, add 5 µL CUT&RUN guinea pig anti-rabbit IgG Negative Contro l corresponding to a 1:20 dilution to the corresponding tube. antibodies-online™ CUT&RUN Negative Control antibodies-online Catalog #ABIN6923140

steps.title: In case you are using one...
steps.instruction: In case you are using one of the CUT&RUN anti-DYKDDDDK antibodies , add 5 µL corresponding to a 1:20 dilution to the corresponding tube.
